objective: Fix the Windows skill-elision path bug in opencodex and complete a repo-wide cross-platform compatibility audit + remediation, using gpt-5.6-sol subagents for survey/review lanes per user request. Repo: /Users/jun/Developer/new/700_projects/opencodex. Concrete outcomes: (1) src/claude/inbound.ts maybeElideSkillText basename extraction must handle both POSIX and Windows separators (split on /[\\/]/), with a regression test proving a Windows path like C:\Users\user\AppData\...\claude-api is correctly elided for blocked skills while POSIX behavior is unchanged; (2) a full cross-platform audit of the codebase — path separator assumptions (split("/"), join with "/", startsWith("/")), node:path vs string ops, hardcoded /tmp or $HOME, env vars (HOME vs USERPROFILE/APPDATA), process spawning/shell assumptions, line endings, case sensitivity — recorded as a devlog unit under devlog/_plan/260715_cross_platform_audit/ with numbered docs (000-range research + decade docs per implementation phase at diff-level, DIFFLEVEL-ROADMAP-01); (3) implementation of every audit-confirmed real defect, each with a test where testable; (4) fresh verification evidence: the repo's typecheck + test suite pass with captured output after each build phase. Out of scope: git push, npm publish/release, unrelated refactors, changing native Anthropic passthrough elision behavior (it intentionally does not truncate). Acceptance/terminal semantics: DONE only when the inbound.ts fix and all audit-confirmed defects are implemented, tests+typecheck pass with fresh captured output, and the devlog unit records findings, fixes, and evidence; BLOCKED/NEEDS_HUMAN if a fix requires a product decision or external access; UNSAFE for anything touching release/publish; BUDGET_EXHAUSTED only against stated bounds (this session's token budget, wall-clock <= a few hours). Verification commands: package.json test/typecheck scripts (bun/npm per repo convention) plus new targeted tests for Windows-path elision. First work-phase: docs-only roadmap cycle (LOOP-DOCS-FIRST-01) producing the audit inventory and diff-level decade docs; the roadmap D locks the goalplan work-phase map; implementation starts the next cycle, one decade doc per PABCD cycle. Git: commit-as-you-go locally (DEV-GIT-COMMIT-01); never push without explicit user approval (DEV-GIT-PUSH-01). Write scope: opencodex repo source/tests/devlog only.
